setting.note: Jackson, Mississippi in the early 1960s, told in the voices of two Black maids, Aibileen and Minny, and Skeeter, a young white woman who secretly collects their accounts of working in white households. The city is real and its divisions are geographic: the white families in the leafy streets of Belhaven, the maids' own neighbourhood over by Farish Street, the segregated downtown, and the Phelan cotton farm out past the edge of town.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Kathryn Stockett, first published 2009. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Help, Kathryn Stockett (2009). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A steady blue against the heat of a Mississippi summer, the colour of the kitchens and back porches where most of the book is told. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
